programme.title: Rosemary Barton Live
programme.desc: Rosemary Barton delivers a weekly dose of compelling conversations and need-to-know political news and issues affecting Canadians.

programme.title: The Fifth Estate
programme.desc: The Fifth Estate brings in-depth investigations important to Canadians, delivering a parade of political leaders, controversial characters and ordinary people with impacted lives.

programme.title: Rosemary Barton Live
programme.desc: Rosemary Barton delivers a weekly dose of compelling conversations and need-to-know political news and issues affecting Canadians.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: CBC News: Morning
programme.desc: The latest news in Canada and around the world.

programme.title: Rosemary Barton Live
programme.desc: Rosemary Barton delivers a weekly dose of compelling conversations and need-to-know political news and issues affecting Canadians.

programme.title: The Fifth Estate
programme.desc: The top-notch team of hosts Bob McKeown, Gillian Findlay, Mark Kelley and Habiba Nosheen provides unique and engaging documentaries from Canada and beyond. "The Fifth Estate" is a reference to the term Fourth Estate, and was chosen to highlight the program's determination to go beyond everyday news into original journalism. The series has won numerous awards for its reporting, including Gemini Awards, International Emmys, the Michener Award, and even an Academy Award for a 1981 episode that was released in theaters the following year. Since September 1975, Canadians have tuned in to experience interesting stories that are always well-crafted and thought-provoking.

programme.title: Rosemary Barton Live
programme.desc: Rosemary Barton delivers a weekly dose of compelling conversations and need-to-know political news and issues affecting Canadians.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: CBC News: Morning
programme.desc: What do you need to know about the world around you? Heather Hiscox takes the helm as the anchor for this weekday-morning news program to tell you. Joining her is Colleen Jones with weather and sports info., and Danielle Bochove with all you need to know about the business world.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: Today's Top Stories
programme.desc: What's happening across Canada and around the world.

programme.title: CTV National News with Heather Butts
programme.sub-title: CTV National News for Saturday, May 3, 2025
programme.desc: Watch CTV National News with Heather Butts

programme.title: CTV News: Your Canada
programme.desc: Stories that showcase what makes Canada unique.

programme.title: CTV News Special: Rick Hansen: Unbreakable 50 Years Later
programme.desc: Rick Hansen returns to the scene of the accident that changed his life forever. CTV’s Sandie Rinaldo talks to the Canadian icon.

programme.title: CTV National News with Heather Butts
programme.sub-title: CTV National News for Saturday, May 3, 2025
programme.desc: Watch CTV National News with Heather Butts

programme.title: CTV News: Your Canada
programme.desc: Stories that showcase what makes Canada unique.

programme.title: W5: Avery Haines Investigates
programme.desc: Avery rides atop Mexico’s “death train” through narco territory, documenting the harrowing journey migrants take in search of a better life.

programme.title: CTV News Special: Rick Hansen: Unbreakable 50 Years Later
programme.desc: Rick Hansen returns to the scene of the accident that changed his life forever. CTV’s Sandie Rinaldo talks to the Canadian icon.

programme.desc: Avery rides atop Mexico’s “death train” through narco territory, documenting the harrowing journey migrants take in search of a better life.

programme.title: CTV News Special: Rick Hansen: Unbreakable 50 Years Later
programme.desc: Rick Hansen returns to the scene of the accident that changed his life forever. CTV’s Sandie Rinaldo talks to the Canadian icon.

programme.title: W5: Avery Haines Investigates

programme.title: Staying Inn: Hotel Julie
programme.desc: Autumn and Jillian begin the renovation of a 19th century inn in Stratford, Ont., starting with the smallest suite in the hotel.

programme.title: Staying Inn: Hotel Julie
programme.desc: The team tackle their first full-sized suite and the construction crew runs into a problem that could affect the entire renovation.

programme.title: Staying Inn: Hotel Julie
programme.sub-title: Double The Room, Double The Fun
programme.desc: Autumn and Jillian face a suite with twice the bathrooms and bedrooms.

programme.title: Staying Inn: Hotel Julie
programme.desc: No light? No problem! Autumn and her team are tasked with making their next suite brighter, while accommodating for more heads in beds.

programme.title: Staying Inn: Hotel Julie
programme.desc: Autumn and Jillian handle a unique suite packed with opportunities, and they take a big risk with an unusual bathroom fixture.

programme.title: Staying Inn: Hotel Julie
programme.desc: The team want to show off their creativity, but a unique doorway, a bold fabric, and construction snafus leave the team in gridlock.

programme.title: Staying Inn: Hotel Julie
programme.desc: Autumn and Jillian get innovative designing a bedroom, while struggling to make the layout and colour palette of Suite 9 work.

programme.title: Staying Inn: Hotel Julie
programme.desc: Autumn and Jillian want to be edgier and moodier than ever for Suite 3, tapping into all things repurposed, vintage, and dramatic.

programme.title: Staying Inn: Hotel Julie
programme.desc: On their final suite, Autumn lives out a design dream, while the duo pulls out all the stops to create their most romantic suite yet.

programme.title: Staying Inn: Hotel Julie
programme.desc: The entire team comes together to complete the guest experience, and finalize the redesign for the grand reveal of Hotel Julie.

programme.title: Country House Hunters
programme.desc: After living in Portland for the past few years, Brian & Gina are looking to relocate to Warrnambool on Victoria East Coast.

programme.title: Country House Hunters
programme.desc: Wendy and Gary are looking to buy a house in beautiful Mount Macedon, Victoria.

programme.title: Country House Hunters
programme.desc: After living in Melbourne for the past few years, Craig is looking to bring his family back to the surf coast where grew up.

programme.title: Country House Hunters
programme.desc: Mark and Jen are looking to buy in the gorgeous Langwarrin area, Victoria.

programme.title: Country House Hunters
programme.desc: Join Catriona Rowntree as we leave the city and enter the beautiful Australian countryside.

programme.title: Country House Hunters
programme.desc: Join Catriona Rowntree as we leave the city and enter the beautiful Australian countryside.

programme.title: Country House Hunters
programme.desc: Catriona helps Julie and Nick, who are looking to move to Mansfield for the stunning mountain views.

programme.title: Country House Hunters
programme.desc: Catriona helps Dirk and Erin, who are looking for a large country retreat.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: The qualifying round ends in the East with eight Atlantic cake makers. Four are chosen to bake their “Bucket List” cake for the judges.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: The top cake makers from each region come together in the CROSS COUNTRY CAKE OFF kitchen to bake for the grand prize in the national finale.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: Bakers from the West present their best cakes to judges Mary Berg and Andrew Han. The top four bake Outdoor Adventure cakes to continue on.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: The Prairies’ top cake makers meet the judges in Edmonton and four are chosen to bring a “Water World” to life for a spot in the finale.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: Mid-Canada’s cake makers present their edible art. The best move on to bake a “Child’s Play” cake in the Cross Country Cake Off kitchen.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs go raw – and raw vegan – with their variations on a classic Japanese dish.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs offer worldwide inspirations on a classic rice dish.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs go raw – and raw vegan – with their variations on a classic Japanese dish.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs offer worldwide inspirations on a classic rice dish.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs begin in East Asia with classic Pad Thai then make twists inspired by the classic.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Bianca Osbourne combines the cuisines of Italy and China to create inventive dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne combines the cuisines of Georgia and Hong Kong to create delicious dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne combines the cuisines of South Korea and Spain to create inventive dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne celebrates the tastes of Morocco and Thailand and creates delicious dishes combining their cuisines.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Wendy Russell makes over our linen closet in 60 minutes; top tips on how to shop online for the best deals!

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Charles the Butler reveals stain-busting tips and techniques; plus, tricks to end the paper pile-up and staying organized.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Relationship advice for a sexless relationship; plus, how to get your kids to help with the chores.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: The Marilyn Denis Show experts weigh in on why belly fat can kill you. Discover belly busting eating guides and belly fat burning exercises.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Checklist for draft-proofing your home for winter; plus, House & Home’s, Suzanne Dimma’s winter chic décor ideas.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: We reveal the 7 most disastrous money disorders and share expert tips to gain financial control and freedom.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Bullying: Take a Stand – hear personal stories and get empowering advice from our experts.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: All you need to know about laser eye surgery; plus Canadian recording artist, Keshia Chanté performs.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Stylish holiday décor tips and tricks with Debbie Travis; Lynn Crawford, shares a recipe from her highly anticipated new cookbook.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: How to choose the perfect Christmas tree and making your fresh seasonal décor last longer; design lessons on how to decorate your bedroom.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Tiny Satellites are growing in main stream popularity, and are being used for interplanetary missions as well.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: It appears that Einstein is once again triumphant as we discover the latest in deep space observations.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: We're looking hard, but the numbers aren't stacking up. Is life on other planets really that hard to find?

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Two new space probes can now orbit closer to the Sun than ever before, enabling groundbreaking research.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Scientists are studying the benefits of a hybrid moon mission, one where humans and robots work in tandem.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Climate change is causing increasing havoc to our environment. How can science respond to this challenge?

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Venus may be considered Earth's evil twin. Hostile and unforgiving, Venus is the subject of intense scrutiny.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The International Space Station is now the only manned station in space, but soon there may be more.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Asteroids are everywhere in space. We count them, examine why they exist, and track them for Earth's safety.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: In our search for life beyond Earth, seeking water on other planetary bodies is an important first step.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The James Webb Space Telescope will be our key to understanding where we fit in our enormous universe.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Time and space converge in black holes. Now we can peer into its abyss to discover its secrets.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Earth's new generation of orbiting telescopes promises to expand our universe of knowledge about our origins.

programme.title: Voyage of Darwin's Beagle: On The Future of Species
programme.desc: About 180 years after Darwin, his great-granddaughter follows his path, traveling from Tenerife to the Amazon.

programme.title: Voyage of Darwin's Beagle: On The Future of Species
programme.sub-title: Low Life on the Ladder of God
programme.desc: Follow Darwin's journey to see remnants of slavery and to relive his discovery of fossils of extinct animals.

programme.title: Expedition Antarctica
programme.desc: A hardy research crew seeks clues about the fate of our planet beneath the icy waters of the Antarctic Ocean.

programme.title: The Jupiter Enigma
programme.desc: NASA’s Juno mission to Jupiter is revealing the origins of our solar system, when Earth's fate was uncertain.

programme.title: The Saturn V Story
programme.desc: This is the story of the most powerful machine ever built and a nation who watched on with hope, together.

programme.title: Mars Calling: Manifest Destiny or Grand Illusion
programme.desc: This film merges the mythic and the scientific to show that Mars may not be what we imagine.

programme.title: The Next Great Extinction Event
programme.desc: There have been five major extinction events in our planet's history, are we about to experience the 6th?

programme.title: Faster Than Light: the Dream of Interstellar Flight
programme.desc: Next-generation rocket technology is fueling the dream of traveling across the void.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: In our search for life beyond Earth, seeking water on other planetary bodies is an important first step.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The James Webb Space Telescope will be our key to understanding where we fit in our enormous universe.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Time and space converge in black holes. Now we can peer into its abyss to discover its secrets.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Earth's new generation of orbiting telescopes promises to expand our universe of knowledge about our origins.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode explores the search for hurricane scientists' holy grail.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode looks at how plants and animals cope with the annual onslaught in the hurricane zone.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: With coastal habitats and natural defenses irretrievably damaged, the future appears bleak.

programme.title: Life in Outer Space
programme.desc: Scientists are exploring planets and moons that may once have supported life. Saturn's moon, Europa, is one.

programme.title: Planet Rethink
programme.desc: Pollution is everywhere. But all is not dire. We need to increase resource efficiency to preserve our planet.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Solar Superstorms: Journey to the Center of the Sun
programme.desc: As technology and electrical grids have spread, big solar storms pose a threat equal to the worst hurricanes.

programme.title: Wild About New Zealand
programme.desc: Gus visits a rugged, remote South Island landscape wedged between the Tasman Sea and the great Southern Alps.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: Stocking up energy and breeding are the major challenges for wild plants and animals that accompany spring.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: For wild animals in Central Europe, early springtime is time to gain energy and produce offspring.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: The traditional mixed orchard is a crucially important cultivated landscape, especially in fall and winter.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: In spring and summer, the orchard meadow reveals an invaluable habitat for the local flora and fauna.

programme.title: Wild About New Zealand
programme.desc: Gus explores Aoraki Mt. Cook National Park, where 140 peaks above 2,000 meters tower over the landscape.

programme.title: Wild About New Zealand
programme.desc: Gus starts his exploration of Tongariro National Park by delving into the myths and lore of the park.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.sub-title: On Safari in Kenya’s Nature Paradise
programme.desc: The Maasai Mara is one of the most breathtaking animal habitats in the world.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: There are few places on earth that are closer to the stars than the high Andes.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: Patagonia seems to be primarily uninhabited by man, which makes this harsh, wild land a paradise for animals.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.sub-title: From Pantanal to the Iguacu Waterfalls
programme.desc: The Pantanal, in the southwest of Brazil, is the largest marsh in the world.

programme.title: Antarctica
programme.desc: Explore Antarctica, one of the world's most beautiful, yet inhospitable, landscapes.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: The West Coast of Madagascar is remote and inhospitable but home to an astonishing array of animals.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: The Northeast of Madagascar is a major trading center and one of the most species-rich regions in the world.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: Madagascar is an island paradise in the Indian Ocean, home to fun-loving folk and a unique natural landscape.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: The Black Forest captivates all who visit and has a reputation for its unique landscape and inhabitants.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.sub-title: Wilderness In the Heart of Europe
programme.desc: The Vosges are home to countless species, encompassing mountains, valleys, forests, and fields.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.sub-title: In the Valley of the Storks
programme.desc: The emblem of The Vosges is the stork. Once gone almost entirely, there are now 400 resident breeding pairs.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.sub-title: On Safari in Kenya’s Nature Paradise
programme.desc: The Maasai Mara is one of the most breathtaking animal habitats in the world.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: There are few places on earth that are closer to the stars than the high Andes.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: Patagonia seems to be primarily uninhabited by man, which makes this harsh, wild land a paradise for animals.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.sub-title: From Pantanal to the Iguacu Waterfalls
programme.desc: The Pantanal, in the southwest of Brazil, is the largest marsh in the world.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: The human imagination is limited when it comes to animal senses.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: On this episode we travel from the desert to the forest to learn how creatures never miss an auditory trick.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: On this episode of Super Senses, we get in touch with the feelings of wild animals.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: On this episode we examine how things taste, and how vital scent is from the animal perspective.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: There are senses we share with most animals, but what about those senses that humans aren't equipped with?

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: There’s a whole world of sensations that animals are aware of but humans are completely unable to detect.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: In this episode, we examine how every animal has what could be called a sense of rhythm.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: While all life began in the oceans, human beings now rely on technology to navigate the seven seas.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: While all life began in the oceans, human beings now rely on technology to navigate the seven seas.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: In this episode we find out more about the animals that live in the shadows.

programme.title: Earth's Survival: Decoding the Science
programme.desc: This documentary explains how we may be in the middle of the most crucial moment of Earth’s history.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Volcanoes can bury entire cities in clouds of ash and gas, or turn rivers into torrents of mud and debris.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: There is no stopping these massive and deadly phenomena, the only thing to do is run to higher ground.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: From the tremendous, to the terrifying, to the tragic, this is a countdown to a mega tornado.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Earthquakes baffle experts as they try to gather the information necessary to predict the next big one.

programme.title: Earth in 1000 years
programme.desc: Earth's stores of ice on Greenland and Antarctica are melting, and sea levels rising. Where is Earth headed?

programme.title: Antarctica
programme.desc: Explore Antarctica, one of the world's most beautiful, yet inhospitable, landscapes.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: An asteroid could be barreling toward Earth at any time, now or in the future. Only science can save us.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Tornadoes: Can we use modern technology to predict the emergence and path of this violent force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Volcanoes: How successful can we be in the fight against this violent, destructive, elemental force of nature?

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Dream Islands of Europe: Lanzarote and Fuerteventura
programme.desc: The film presents the two Canary Islands Lanzarote and Fuerteventura. Lanzarote has a rich culture in addition to a volcanic landscape: the film accompanies a ceramic artist, an instrument maker, shows architecture by César Manrique and a camel breeder. On Fuerteventura, in addition to livestock farming and the operation of traditional grain mills, nature conservation plays a major role: islanders are committed to preserving the sand dunes, and there is a turtle hatchery. On Lanzarote, countless volcanic eruptions have formed a bizarre lunar landscape. It is a great challenge for the inhabitants to extract the cultivation of food from the karstic, dry soil. And yet they even manage to produce wine. Protected by thousands of small depressions that act as water reservoirs, they grow vines on porous volcanic rock.

programme.title: mareTV: Tenerife - The Queen of the Canaries
programme.desc: Tenerife is outstanding among the Canary Islands. If only because of the volcano Pico del Teide, which rises exactly 3.718 meters above sea level and is thus the highest mountain in Spain. But not only scenically Tenerife, the largest of the Canary Islands, stands out...

programme.title: Fuerteventura: The golden beauty of the Canaries
programme.desc: Fuerteventura, the second largest of the Canary Islands, is located just 120 kilometres off the coast of West Africa. The climate is extremely dry and the struggle for water has always been a major setback. Then, around a century ago, returning US emigrants brought what were then modern wind turbine water pumps from the Aermotor Windmill Company with them to Fuerte-ventura. Today, the environmentally friendly and so-called “Chicago’s”, experience a renaissance. Unfortu-nately, original spare parts in good condition are diffi-cult to come by. When Leocadio Araya Sanchez began planting countless cacti on a barren hillside a few years ago, people thought he was mad. His idea was to make a healthy juice drink from the prickly pears – with great success. But getting the fruit to release its pre-cious superfood juice is no simple task. On an almost daily basis, Marcos Tapia is magnetically attracted to one of the paradisiacal beaches of the island. The 44-year-old is Spanish champion and vice world champion in para-body board surfing. When he was 28. Marcos was involved in a serious motorbike accident and has been paraplegic ever since.

programme.title: The Secret of Migrating Birds - Tryst at the Red Sea
programme.desc: Each year in spring, Israel witnesses a unique spectacle. Many millions of migrating birds cross the land bridge between Africa, Asia and Europe.Their first major roosting place after an exhausting nonstop flight over the African deserts is in southern Israel – a vital stopover. Here they replenish their energy reserves. The flocks of migrating birds are pursued by flocks of ornithologists. Hundreds of birders flood into the port city of Eilat on the Red Sea to take part in the Migration Festival. For a week, Israel's southernmost coastal resort is home to international scientists and amateur birders.

programme.title: King of the Mountains - Golden Eagle
programme.desc: Aerial shots of mating Golden eagles, a clash between a Golden and a White-tailed eagle, and an intimate glimpse into their nesting grounds combine to create a spectacular film. Tracking these majestic birds reveals the wild nature of their hunting grounds, showcasing the diverse animal and plant life that share their habitat with the 'king of the skies.' Viewers are brought up close as the eagle effortlessly glides over forests and canyons, abruptly diving in a breathtaking pursuit of prey. Witnessing these birds in their natural habitats is witnessing them at their finest. Embark on a magnificent journey through the skies and landscapes with us.

programme.title: King of the Seas - Sea Eagle
programme.desc: The times where eagles have been seen as varmints are over. They are now perceived as an integral part of the food cycle. Due to the fact that the habitats of the sea eagles in Germany are largely found in the north-eastern regions that are rich in water, the movie accompanies the eagles to an island, home to hundreds of sea ravens, where they rob the baby sea ravens of their food. They seem to always profit from the successes of the fellow creatures in their habitats. This film introduces you to the expert poachers of the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Hummingbirds: Jewelled Messengers
programme.desc: Hummingbirds live at the limits of what is possible. They have the highest metabolism with a heart rate of 1,200 beats per minute of any vertebrate. Their wings beat 50 times a second. They can hover on the spot, and even fly backwards. To our eyes, they are just a blur. But with the aid of high-speed cameras we can slow down their hyperactive lives and look into their secret world.

programme.title: Snowy Owl: Queen of the North
programme.desc: No other owls are capable of surviving the extreme conditions, whereas the snowy owl is able to hunt through six months of bright sunshine and six months of twilight. This film follows a pair of snowy owls as they breed on the tundra of the High Arctic, 300 miles inside the Arctic Circle. Lemming numbers are low this year. Five owlets hatch and they face a struggle if any are going to survive.

programme.title: Missing Butterflies
programme.desc: Its miraculous transformation from caterpillar to remarkable winged beauty has been a point of constant intrigue. However, in the last few years, many have observed a decrease in the butterfly population. A much-discussed topic in the entomological society is the decline of the insect populace, but to what extent is this topic relevant to our beloved butterflies? For years, a teacher from lower Bavaria and a biologist from Munich have dedicated themselves to studying the butterfly and their presence in Bavaria. The results of their studies tell an enlightening story that is sure to entertain.

programme.title: Unnatural Selection
programme.desc: The wings of cliff swallows in Nebraska are getting shorter – while female turtles in Chesapeake Bay are getting larger. On the Galapagos Islands, two species of finches are developing back into one. Increasing numbers of peregrine falcons are living in urban areas – and have adapted their behaviour: thanks to the city lights, they have learned to hunt at night.

programme.title: Leap Of The Lynx
programme.desc: On the Iberian Peninsula, primeval landscapes are practically non-existent, so the Iberian lynx has lived on cultivated land for centuries – cattle, horse and pig pastures, cork oak forests, timberland, and even holy sites of pilgrimage. The diversity and beauty of these locations are truly stunning and their character and history as relevant to this story as that of the big cat itself. Until five years ago, both populations of Iberian lynx were quickly fading away. Their fragmented habitats were shrinking even further, they were starved by lack of prey and reduced by epidemics. But now, local authorities, scientists, landowners, and other groups have finally begun to act in unison.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Kevin Richardson shares an extraordinary bond with Africa’s most fearsome predators. Together with them, he has experienced some real adventures. However, transporting 27 lions and 14 hyenas to a new home presents a different challenge.The lions' new home will be a sanctuary on the edge of Dinokeng, a South African game reserve spanning 18,000 square metres. The huge task ahead includes planning and designing the new enclosures, and securing permits to move the animals.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Not only are the animals regularly rotated into new enclosures to prevent repetitive behaviour developing out of boredom, but Kevin also has to practice loading them in and out of the van. Can the reluctant lions and hyenas eventually be persuaded to get on board with the move?

programme.title: Elephants: Back to the Wild
programme.sub-title: Elephants: Back to the Wild: The Orphanage of Elephants - Nairobi Nationalpark
programme.desc: We follow the rescue of a baby elephant. Once rescued, it will be transported by plane to the breeding station and raised with love and commitment. In order to satisfy the little pachyderm’s urge to move while in the breeding station, there is sometimes an adorable football match between the animals.

programme.title: Elephants: Back to the Wild
programme.sub-title: Elephants: Back to the Wild: The Return of the Elephants - Tsavo-East-Nationalpark
programme.desc: We accompany the first steps of a two-year-old elephant who is just starting his release program. We also observe the story of two young elephants in their first night in the wilderness on their own. Captivating film footage allows us to truly learn how elephants behave in the wild.

programme.title: Rewilding: Back to Nature
programme.desc: Worldwide, there are various projects and approaches to give nature more space again. The aim is to create larger wilderness areas that develop with hardly any influence of humans - by reintroducing so-called key animal species and creating a network of wilderness corridors across entire continents. Rewilding promises to be a cost-effective form of conservation in which we can boost biodiversity and protect the climate.

programme.title: Wild Prairie Man
programme.desc: Located in the Northern Great Plains of Canada, Grasslands National Park has been a 20 year odyssey for wildlife photographer James R. Page. For James, critters are more interesting than people and he shares some of his iconic images and experiences from this endangered ecosystem. We showcase the parks wilderness that is dominated by prairie light and dark sky, the wildlife, prairie dogs, mesmerizing landscapes, and bisons. We immerse ourselves in the wild habitat of Grasslands National Park for over a year capturing some never before scenes in the Wild Prairie. James R. Page's connection to the land and the wildlife provides an unforgettable journey into the heart of this endangered ecosystem, while Park experts help us understand its complexity.

programme.title: The Secret of Migrating Birds - Tryst at the Red Sea
programme.desc: Each year in spring, Israel witnesses a unique spectacle. Many millions of migrating birds cross the land bridge between Africa, Asia and Europe.Their first major roosting place after an exhausting nonstop flight over the African deserts is in southern Israel – a vital stopover. Here they replenish their energy reserves. The flocks of migrating birds are pursued by flocks of ornithologists. Hundreds of birders flood into the port city of Eilat on the Red Sea to take part in the Migration Festival. For a week, Israel's southernmost coastal resort is home to international scientists and amateur birders.

programme.title: King of the Mountains - Golden Eagle
programme.desc: Aerial shots of mating Golden eagles, a clash between a Golden and a White-tailed eagle, and an intimate glimpse into their nesting grounds combine to create a spectacular film. Tracking these majestic birds reveals the wild nature of their hunting grounds, showcasing the diverse animal and plant life that share their habitat with the 'king of the skies.' Viewers are brought up close as the eagle effortlessly glides over forests and canyons, abruptly diving in a breathtaking pursuit of prey. Witnessing these birds in their natural habitats is witnessing them at their finest. Embark on a magnificent journey through the skies and landscapes with us.

programme.title: King of the Seas - Sea Eagle
programme.desc: The times where eagles have been seen as varmints are over. They are now perceived as an integral part of the food cycle. Due to the fact that the habitats of the sea eagles in Germany are largely found in the north-eastern regions that are rich in water, the movie accompanies the eagles to an island, home to hundreds of sea ravens, where they rob the baby sea ravens of their food. They seem to always profit from the successes of the fellow creatures in their habitats. This film introduces you to the expert poachers of the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Hummingbirds: Jewelled Messengers
programme.desc: Hummingbirds live at the limits of what is possible. They have the highest metabolism with a heart rate of 1,200 beats per minute of any vertebrate. Their wings beat 50 times a second. They can hover on the spot, and even fly backwards. To our eyes, they are just a blur. But with the aid of high-speed cameras we can slow down their hyperactive lives and look into their secret world.

programme.title: Snowy Owl: Queen of the North
programme.desc: No other owls are capable of surviving the extreme conditions, whereas the snowy owl is able to hunt through six months of bright sunshine and six months of twilight. This film follows a pair of snowy owls as they breed on the tundra of the High Arctic, 300 miles inside the Arctic Circle. Lemming numbers are low this year. Five owlets hatch and they face a struggle if any are going to survive.

programme.title: Missing Butterflies
programme.desc: Its miraculous transformation from caterpillar to remarkable winged beauty has been a point of constant intrigue. However, in the last few years, many have observed a decrease in the butterfly population. A much-discussed topic in the entomological society is the decline of the insect populace, but to what extent is this topic relevant to our beloved butterflies? For years, a teacher from lower Bavaria and a biologist from Munich have dedicated themselves to studying the butterfly and their presence in Bavaria. The results of their studies tell an enlightening story that is sure to entertain.

programme.title: Unnatural Selection
programme.desc: The wings of cliff swallows in Nebraska are getting shorter – while female turtles in Chesapeake Bay are getting larger. On the Galapagos Islands, two species of finches are developing back into one. Increasing numbers of peregrine falcons are living in urban areas – and have adapted their behaviour: thanks to the city lights, they have learned to hunt at night.

programme.title: Leap Of The Lynx
programme.desc: On the Iberian Peninsula, primeval landscapes are practically non-existent, so the Iberian lynx has lived on cultivated land for centuries – cattle, horse and pig pastures, cork oak forests, timberland, and even holy sites of pilgrimage. The diversity and beauty of these locations are truly stunning and their character and history as relevant to this story as that of the big cat itself. Until five years ago, both populations of Iberian lynx were quickly fading away. Their fragmented habitats were shrinking even further, they were starved by lack of prey and reduced by epidemics. But now, local authorities, scientists, landowners, and other groups have finally begun to act in unison.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Kevin Richardson shares an extraordinary bond with Africa’s most fearsome predators. Together with them, he has experienced some real adventures. However, transporting 27 lions and 14 hyenas to a new home presents a different challenge.The lions' new home will be a sanctuary on the edge of Dinokeng, a South African game reserve spanning 18,000 square metres. The huge task ahead includes planning and designing the new enclosures, and securing permits to move the animals.

programme.title: Eagle - Queen Of The Skies
programme.desc: To explore the world of the eagle we follow the fortunes of one individual from hatching to leaving the nest.

programme.title: Adopted By Dolphins - Flipper's Family Secrets
programme.desc: Researchers are accepted as companions by a population of bottlenose dolphins in the Red Sea

programme.title: Hamilton’s Journey - Manatees in a New Light
programme.desc: A manatee has to be extremely unlucky to be given a name. Hamilton is a living example of this.

programme.title: Yellowstone Journals
programme.desc: Yellowstone's spring melt reveals animals gently guiding newborns to learn the world.

programme.title: Yellowstone Journals
programme.desc: Yellowstone's creatures thrive due to unique abilities, anatomies, and senses, especially in Spring.

programme.title: Yellowstone Journals
programme.desc: Yellowstone's creatures thrive with unique abilities, anatomies, and senses, especially in Spring.

programme.title: World of Oceans
programme.desc: Sharks, often feared, have a deeper significance beyond their appearance.

programme.title: World of Oceans
programme.desc: All creatures aim to pass on their genes and ensure the success of their offspring.

programme.title: World of Oceans
programme.desc: The ocean's mysteries and creatures have inspired myths and legends for thousands of years.

programme.title: Wild Galapagos Series 01 Episode 01
programme.desc: The Galapagos Archipelago's diverse biodiversity is attributed to the Pacific's Humboldt

programme.title: Wild Galapagos Series 01 Episode 02
programme.desc: home to diverse creatures, inspired Charles Darwin's evolution theory due to their unique adaptations

programme.title: Aerial Assassins
programme.desc: James Currie is embarking on a mission to witness the majestic Harris Hawk

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Animals like toads, moths, snakes, and spiders have poison glands

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: The archer fish uses a blowgun to capture insects, while the jawfish defends itself by throwing boulders.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Cuttlefish and squid use black fluid to hide from predators, while skunks also use scent glands for defense.

programme.title: Otters: River Masters of Yellowstone
programme.desc: Follow two families of river otters in Yellowstone and how they join together to face small challenges.

programme.title: Eagle - Queen Of The Skies
programme.desc: To explore the world of the eagle we follow the fortunes of one individual from hatching to leaving the nest.

programme.title: Adopted By Dolphins - Flipper's Family Secrets
programme.desc: Researchers are accepted as companions by a population of bottlenose dolphins in the Red Sea

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef 4K
programme.desc: Dive into the Great Barrier Reef in stunning 4K, exploring vibrant coral, turtles, and fish in one of Earth's most diverse natural wonders.

programme.title: Wild Slovakia
programme.desc: Wild Slovakia is a documentary film showcasing Slovakia's diverse wildlife, habitats, and ecosystems

programme.title: Masters Of The Wild
programme.desc: Animals adapt to hostile landscapes using specialized bodies, diets, and behaviors, such as scorpions

programme.title: Beak & Brain - Genius Birds From Down Under
programme.desc: These child prodigies of the animal kingdom have feathers and beaks - and very special skills!

programme.title: Punda the Zebra
programme.desc: Following zebra foal Punda from birth and how it learns skills needed to survive in the African savanna.

programme.title: Megeti
programme.desc: A lone Ethiopian Wolf battles the elements and other predators as she tries to unite with a pack.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Kevin Richardson, animal lover, faces the challenge of relocating 28 lions and 14 hyenas.

programme.title: The Unknown Snake Of Bolivia
programme.desc: Herpetologists Juan Tims and Hiram Cuevas explore Bolivia to find venomous snakes and a new viper.

programme.title: Wild Morocco Monkeys And Snakes
programme.desc: Documentary highlights Morocco's unique wildlife, featuring Barbary apes & rare reptiles in their habitats.

programme.title: Magical Reef: The Islands of the Four Kings
programme.desc: Explore Indonesia's underwater paradise, home to vibrant coral reefs and unique sea life species.

programme.title: The Fifth Estate
programme.desc: The Fifth Estate brings in-depth investigations that matter to Canadians.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: The National is CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth journalism.

programme.title: CBC News: Atlantic Tonight
programme.desc: Host Amy Smith brings you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: CBC News: Atlantic Tonight
programme.desc: Host Amy Smith brings you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: The National is CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth journalism.

programme.title: CBC News: Atlantic Tonight
programme.desc: Host Amy Smith brings you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: CBC News: Atlantic Tonight
programme.desc: Host Amy Smith brings you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: The National is CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth journalism.

programme.title: CBC News: Atlantic Tonight
programme.desc: Host Amy Smith brings you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Survivorman
programme.desc: Les travels by plane, bus, boat and foot to reach the Huacharia Tribe that still hunts with bow and arrow

programme.title: Survivorman
programme.desc: Les climbs up dangerous Mt. Sinkara to join indigenous Andeans as they make their pilgrimage to Qollorit'i.

programme.title: What If
programme.desc: Take an epic journey through hypothetical worlds and possibilities.

programme.title: Crazy Creatures
programme.desc: Evolution has crafted some of the strangest beings you can imagine, and it's all been done for a reason.

programme.title: Survivorman: Bigfoot
programme.desc: Whoops and strange noises from the forest interior leave Les unsettled yet skeptical.

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Hidden Spain
programme.desc: Spain is found at the crossroads between the Mediterranean and the Atlantic, resulting in diverse landscapes, ancient cultures and magnificent wildlife.

programme.title: Wild and Alive
programme.desc: In the heart of the Ivory Coast a small rural village has a very special resident. The last white rhino in the country sleeps on the village outskirts and feeds on the villagers' crops.

programme.title: Wild and Alive
programme.desc: On the savannah of southern Africa, rains have turned the land green and lush. It's the time of plenty and for the cast of wild characters here, cashing in is priority number one.

programme.title: Seals
programme.desc: Life is rarely easy for seals, and yet all over the world they consistently rise to the challenge – and always have fun doing it.

programme.title: Mind of a Giant
programme.desc: What is it like to be an elephant? This series reveals new research which attempts to answer that question.

programme.title: Seals
programme.desc: Life is rarely easy for seals, and yet all over the world they consistently rise to the challenge – and always have fun doing it.

programme.title: Wild Portugal
programme.desc: Although Portugal is a country with a relatively small territory, the difference of landscapes is quite notorious.

programme.title: Destination Wild: Wild Islands
programme.desc: Zanzibar is a land of giants. Isolated from mainland Africa these islands harbour some of the most unique animals with many being the largest found anywhere in the world.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Take a look far, far away to the realm of Alien Galaxies.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: How will we live... in space?

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: What are the latest discoveries in the deadly world of asteroids?

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Are the rings of Saturn a real celestial phenomenon or merely a cosmic Illusion?

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Scientists and astronomers create five lines of extraterrestrial evolution.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Outer space is already an essential part of America's ability to fight wars.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Stunning rings of Saturn have mesmerized countless scientists over the centuries.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Take a look far, far away to the realm of Alien Galaxies.
